dimensions.name: Question clarity — generic & clean, no use-cases or analogies
dimensions.description: Is the question simple, direct, generic and clean, understandable in one reading? Questions MUST NOT be framed around use-cases, scenarios, stories, or analogies — they should ask directly about the concept/term itself.

anchors.1: The question is built from a use-case/scenario/story or an analogy, OR is confusing/complex, OR asks about topics outside the material.
anchors.5: The question is mostly clear and generic but leans slightly on a scenario/example or has some ambiguous phrasing.
anchors.10: The question is immediately clear, generic and clean — it asks directly about the concept/term with no use-case, scenario, story, or analogy framing. Readable in one pass.

dimensions.name: Option quality — equal length, material-only words, one correct
dimensions.description: Are all four options plausible but only one correct? Are ALL options (including the WRONG ones) built ONLY from words/terminology present in the provided material/PPT — no external terms? Are all four options roughly EQUAL in length so length never hints at the answer? Wrong options must not be partial-correct consequences/symptoms, and must contain no analogies.

anchors.1: Wrong options introduce terms/tools/concepts NOT in the material, OR option lengths are clearly unequal (correct one longer/shorter), OR options contain analogies or partial-correct consequences.
anchors.5: Options are mostly plausible but one or two introduce a term not found in the material, OR there is a noticeable length imbalance.
anchors.10: Only one correct answer. EVERY option — including all distractors — uses ONLY words/terminology from the material (nothing external). All four options are roughly equal in length so length gives nothing away. No analogies; wrong options are genuinely incorrect, not partial consequences.

dimensions.name: Material alignment — only session content tested
dimensions.description: Does the question test only concepts explicitly covered in the session? Are terminology, examples, and contexts taken from the session material?

anchors.1: The question tests concepts not covered in the session (e.g. asks about software applications when it was not explained, references external tools or techniques never mentioned, introduces new terminology).
anchors.5: The question is mostly aligned to session content but uses one or two terms or examples that were not explicitly covered.
anchors.10: Every word in the question, options, and explanation is grounded in the session material. No external concepts. Exact terminology from the session is used where relevant.

dimensions.name: Question specificity — includes context
dimensions.description: Does the question include enough context (technique name, framework name, domain, node name) so it is standalone and specific? Vague generic questions score low.

anchors.1: Question is generic and vague — e.g. 'What does this do?' or 'What is the main purpose?' with no context. Could apply to any topic.
anchors.5: Question has some context but could be made more specific by naming the technique, tool, or framework being tested.
anchors.10: Question is fully specific. Includes the name of the tool, node, technique, or framework. A student can answer without guessing what context is being referred to.

dimensions.name: Question type and format correctness
dimensions.description: Is the correct QUESTION_TYPE used? Does the format match the prompt specification? Are all required fields present? Does -END- appear at the end?

anchors.1: Wrong QUESTION_TYPE used (e.g. MORE_THAN_ONE_MULTIPLE_CHOICE used when only one answer is correct). Required fields missing. -END- missing. QUESTION_KEY is duplicate or missing.
anchors.5: Format is mostly correct but one field is wrong or missing — e.g. TRUE_FALSE question formatted as MULTIPLE_CHOICE, or LEARNING_OUTCOME is too verbose.
anchors.10: Correct QUESTION_TYPE for the number of correct answers. All required fields present and correctly filled. QUESTION_KEY is unique. LEARNING_OUTCOME is 3-5 words in lowercase_snake_case. -END- present.

dimensions.name: Explanation quality
dimensions.description: Does the explanation clearly justify the correct answer using session terminology? Does it address why wrong options are incorrect?

anchors.1: Explanation is missing, too short (e.g. just restates the correct option), or uses terminology not from the session.
anchors.5: Explanation covers the correct answer but does not explain why the wrong options are incorrect.
anchors.10: Explanation clearly justifies why the correct answer is right using session-specific terminology. Mentions key reasons why each wrong option is incorrect.

dimensions.name: Bloom's level alignment
dimensions.description: Does the question target an appropriate Bloom's level, and does the set vary levels (not all recall)? Is BLOOM_LEVEL set correctly for the question's verb/intent?

anchors.1: All questions are rote recall, or BLOOM_LEVEL is mislabelled relative to what the question actually asks.
anchors.5: Most questions sit at a sensible level but the set lacks variety, or one label does not match the question's cognitive demand.
anchors.10: Each question targets an appropriate Bloom's level, the set spans more than one level, and every BLOOM_LEVEL label matches the question's actual demand.

dimensions.name: Technical accuracy
dimensions.description: Are the stem, options, designated correct answer(s), and explanation all technically correct with respect to the source material?

anchors.1: The question, the keyed-correct option, or the explanation is technically wrong or contradicts the material.
anchors.5: Largely correct but with a minor imprecision in an option or the explanation.
anchors.10: Stem, all options, the correct answer(s), and the explanation are technically accurate and consistent with the material.
